author: @sailingoctopus1
text:
A lot of fear-crazed comments on here in response to a very measured and wise video.

Fear is not a good decision-maker. Our flight-flight response has evolved to make us respond to fear. It hasn't evolved to help us make the right decision it has evolved to make ANY decisions, because any decisions is better than no decision.

For most individuals the decision on whether or not to take the vaccine doesn't matter at all, as they are of very low risk of an adverse reaction to Covid infection or the vaccine.

Considering the needs of others is important. I fearlessly took the vaccine, because I considered the relative risks and it was a no-brainer for me at the age of 61, but I would have done the same had I been 21, because I believe that taking the vaccine lowers the risks for others.

I view mask-wearing in the same light. I personally believe that the time for mask-wearing is over in the UK, owing to the high levels of vaccination, but I wear masks not out of a desire to protect those that have chosen to remain unvaccinated, but out of respect for the feelings of those that are fearful of catching the virus and believe that those that aren't wearing a mask are exposing them to greater risk - I believe they are wrong and that, actually, to those that are vaccinated, encountering the virus at this stage will further boost their immune system. 

I no longer feel obligated to help protect unvaccinated people because they have made their choice and I respect their right to make that choice, but having made that choice I have no further part to play in the equation.

The problem Robert is that in assessing Risk one has to have access to Data that one is fairly confident is correct. How can one assess risk when that data is not only skewed but actively surpressed by both the Government and the Media?
